latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 11:10
RNS Number : 2458W
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
11 March 2026
THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED BY THE COMPANY TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014, AS AMENDED WHICH, BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, FORMS PART OF UK LAW. ON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE ("RIS"), THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
11 March 2026
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
("Sealand" or the "Company")
Issue of Equity
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited (LSE: SCGL) announces that it has entered into a subscription agreement with Mr. Siqi Cao, an Executive Director of the Company, pursuant to which Mr. Cao has conditionally agreed to subscribe for 444,371,233 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each in the capital of the Company (the "Subscription Shares") at a price of £0.001 per Subscription Share (the "Subscription Price"), raising gross proceeds of approximately £444,371.23 (the "Subscription").
Related Party Transaction
Mr. Siqi Cao is an Executive Director of the Company and is therefore considered a related party for the purposes of Rule 7.3 of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. Accordingly, the Subscription constitutes a material related party.
Mr. Cao did not participate in the Board's consideration of the Subscription or vote on the relevant Board resolution.
The independent Directors of the Company, being all Directors other than Mr. Cao, consider, having exercised reasonable care, skill and diligence, that the terms of the Subscription are fair and reasonable insofar as the shareholders of the Company who are not related parties are concerned.
The Board believes that the Subscription provides the Company with additional working capital and demonstrates continued support for the Company's strategy from a senior executive director
Issue of Shares
The Subscription and the issue of the Subscription Shares are conditional on Admission. The Company already has authority to issue the Subscription Shares, which was granted at its AGM on 10 January 2025 and remains in force.
At the date of this announcement, the Company had 1,011,534,756 ordinary shares in issue. If the Subscription completes in full, the Subscription Shares would represent approximately 30.5 per cent. of the Company's enlarged issued share capital.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application will be made for the Subscription Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market of the London Stock Exchange plc ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the Subscription Shares will commence at 8.00am on or around 16
March 2026.
Following Admission, the total issued share capital of the Company will consist of 1,455,905,989 Ordinary Shares. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 1,455,905,989 and this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest, in the share capital of the Company.
Elena Law, Chairwoman of Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited, commented:
"We are pleased to see this continued commitment to the Company from one of our executive directors. The Board believes that securing additional working capital in a timely and cost-efficient manner is important as Sealand continues to develop its business and evaluate opportunities for growth. This subscription reflects confidence in the Company and our focus on building long-term value for shareholders."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
Enquiries:

latest.title: Notice of General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 16:54
RNS Number : 7412V
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
06 March 2026
6
March 2026
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
("Sealand" or the "Company")
Notice of General Meeting
Sealand (LSE: SCGL) announces that it has today published a circular (the "Circular") containing a notice convening a general meeting of the Company (the "General Meeting").
The General Meeting will be held at 11.00 a.m. on
26
March 2026 at the office of Hill Dickinson LLP at The 8th Floor, The Broadgate Tower, 20 Primrose Street, London, EC2A 2EW.
At the General Meeting, shareholders will be asked to consider and, if thought fit, pass:
·
Resolution 1 (Ordinary): increase the Company's authorised share capital from 6,560,000,000 shares of £0.0001 each to 30,000,000,000 shares of £0.0001 each;
·
Resolution 2 (Ordinary): grant the Directors authority to allot shares and grant rights up to an aggregate nominal amount of £2,700,000, expiring on the first anniversary of the General Meeting (
26
March 2027); and
·
Resolution 3 (Special): disapply pre-emption rights (in accordance with Article 6 of the Company's articles) up to an aggregate nominal amount of £2,700,000, expiring on the first anniversary of the General Meeting (
26
March 2027).
The Circular and notice convening the General Meeting, has been posted to all registered shareholders. A form of proxy for holders of ordinary shares is enclosed with the Circular.
Copies of the Circular will shortly be available for inspection on the National Storage Mechanism and will also be made available on the Company's website at
https://www.sealandcapitalgalaxy.com/investors
.

latest.title: Strategic Update and Business Expansion
latest.content:
27 Jan 2026 16:00
RNS Number : 6002Q
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
27 January 2026
THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED BY THE COMPANY TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014, AS AMENDED WHICH, BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, FORMS PART OF UK LAW. ON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE ("RIS"), THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
("Sealand" or the "Company")
Strategic
Update
and Business Expansion
The Board of Sealand (LSE: SCGL) is pleased to announce its intention to
enhance
the Company's strategic objectives and core business operations. The Board believes that focusing the Company's resources on high-growth technology and AI-led opportunities is the best route to enhance long-term shareholder value. Following a review of market trends and the Company's capabilities, the Board intends to
upgrade
and augment the Company's developmental priorities (the "Expansion").
Strategic focus: technology and AI
Under the intended Expansion, the Company plans to prioritise:
·
AI and SaaS software tools to support commercial deployment of digital management solutions; and
·
Computing-power infrastructure and energy technology, with the aim of building a more integrated platform spanning digitalisation, intelligence, computing support and energy assurance.
The Board believes this renewed focus will strengthen the Company's competitiveness and support sustainable revenue generation over time.
Geographic focus: Asia-Pacific
The Company intends to prioritise the Asia-Pacific region through a dual-hub approach:
·
Shenzhen as the hub for Mainland China operations (Greater Bay Area); and
·
Hong Kong as a base for expansion into Southeast Asia and broader Asian markets.
Supporting activities
To support the execution of the Expansion, the Company aims to:
·
provide advisory services to international companies entering China and the UK market; and
·
pursue selective investments aligned with the Company's technology and AI strategy where the Board considers these accelerate delivery of the intended priorities.
Governance and implementation
The Board intends to ensure it has an appropriate mix of skills and experience to oversee the Company's intended technological and infrastructure priorities and may evaluate synergistic M&A opportunities consistent with the Expansion.
All actions will be undertaken in accordance with applicable UK regulatory requirements, including the UK Listing Rules and the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. The Company will provide further updates as appropriate.
For the avoidance of doubt, this announcement sets out the Company's intended strategic direction only and does not constitute a profit forecast or investment commitment.
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
Enquiries:
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
Dr. Thomas Sawyer (Chief Executive Officer)
Mr. Siqi Cao (Executive Director)
Bowsprit Partners Limited (Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: SCGL is a UK‑based specialist in manufacturing and supplying advanced composite materials for aerospace, automotive and renewable‑energy sectors. Its product line includes carbon‑fiber laminates, thermoplastic composites and related tooling.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Composite tech is a high‑growth niche because the aerospace and EV industries are shifting to lighter, stronger parts to improve fuel efficiency and performance. SCGL’s expertise gives it a foothold in contracts with major OEMs and a technology pipeline that could drive future revenue streams.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is currently in a restructuring phase: new directors and a CFO have been appointed, an equity issue was announced, and a strategic update suggests expansion into new markets. Cash burn remains tight, but the company is actively seeking capital and new contracts to stabilize operations.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a series of governance changes—new board members and a CFO—followed by an equity issuance that indicates a fresh capital raise. The strategic update and business‑expansion note hint at a pivot toward larger, longer‑term contracts.

Two themes emerge: (1) a deliberate effort to shore up capital and leadership, and (2) a push to diversify the customer base beyond its current niche. Together they suggest the company is trying to turn a distressed position into a growth platform.
The narrative is one of cautious optimism: the company is not just surviving but actively restructuring, which could create a catalyst if the new capital is deployed effectively.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but wary – the chatter is mostly neutral or mildly bearish, with no real buzz or hype.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders note the new strategic direction and the fresh funding as a sign that the company may rebound once it lands larger contracts.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish voices point to the heavy equity dilution, the lack of recent revenue growth, and the uncertainty around the new business focus.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from pure skepticism to a cautious ‘watch‑and‑wait’ stance, as the latest RNS suggest the firm is taking concrete steps to address its financial issues.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public search interest, which is a classic contrarian green‑light for a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A successful closing of the equity round or a clear allocation of the proceeds to a high‑margin contract could trigger a price rally.
An announcement of a new customer agreement in the automotive or renewable‑energy sector would validate the strategic shift.
Volume spikes that break the 50‑EMA on a low‑float stock would signal institutional buying.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the next quarterly earnings for revenue growth, a reduction in the debt‑to‑cash‑flow ratio, and any sign that the new capital is being used to secure multi‑year contracts. A sudden uptick in short‑interest could also be a warning sign.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a deep‑capitulation cycle – the price is still far below historic lows and the APEX score indicates tight compression. A mean‑reversion bounce is likely once the company shows tangible progress on its restructuring agenda.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company can deploy its fresh capital into a sizable contract, the price could climb to roughly the midpoint of its 52‑week range, yielding a 300‑400% upside on the current trading level. The deep crash (over 70% off its peak) offers a high reward with a low probability of a small loss – a classic asymmetric play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Failure to close new deals, continued dilution, or a liquidity crunch could push the stock back into a lower valuation band. The worst case is a further slide to a new support level, potentially erasing the current market cap entirely.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the entry price (plus a small margin for slippage). Potential gain could be 3–4 times the entry price if the company hits the 52‑week midpoint. The asymmetry is roughly 1:3 to 1:4.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a breakout above the 50‑EMA with volume at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Allocate 10–15% of the discretionary capital per trade and set a stop just below the recent swing low to protect against a short‑term pullback.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position at the current level until you see a clear sign of new capital deployment or a sizable contract announcement. Watch for a volume spike that breaks the 50‑EMA or a 10‑day moving‑average crossover.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy warnings. The only terminal risk is a failure to secure new contracts.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SCGL sits at a deep crash level with a silent crowd and a fresh capital push; if the restructuring delivers a new contract, the upside could be 3‑4× the entry price, making it a high‑reward, low‑probability contrarian play.
